title: /kernel:run
summary_text: Findings: - `Kernel` bootstrap is non-optional: start as multi-lane orchestration, not plain single-thread work. - Required minimum startup shape: at least **6 materially distinct active lanes** before substantive analysis. - Required voices: - `codex` (sovereign, mandatory) - `glm` (mandatory for normal shape) - one specialist from `gemini-cli`, `cursor-cli`, or `copilot-cli` - `degraded-allowed` is allowed only after `glm` has failed twice in the same run, via `codex-kernel-guard glm-fail ...`, and only then as `codex + specialist + specialist`. - If normal or degraded shape cannot be established, mode is `BLOCKED`; do not de-rail below minimum. - Before first acknowledgement, write bootstrap receipt: - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh write <lane_count> <providers_csv> <normal|degraded-allowed>` - Must set env: - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_ACTIVE_MODELS_CSV` - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_MANIFEST_LANE_COUNT` - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_AGENT_LABELS=true` - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_SUBAGENT_LABELS=true` - Required acknowledgement header: - `Kernel orchestration is active for this session.` - `Bootstrap target: 6+ lanes (minimum 6).` - `Active models: <main model>, <diversity model>, <diversity model> ...` - `Lane manifest:` - Lane manifest must be at least 6 bullets, for active lanes only, each exactly in: - `- <lane name>: <role> - <focus> [provider:<provider>] [agent:<agent>] [subagent:<subagent1|subagent2|...|none>]` - `Active models:` and manifest must reflect live evidence from current run, not assumptions or planned threads. Blockers: - Cannot claim healthy bootstrap unless 6+ active lanes exist with live evidence. - Cannot claim active manifest on pending/failed/planned lanes; those do not count. - Cannot default to fewer lanes or single-model execution after startup.
